bead.title: server: eliminate panicking root GraphQL Query resolvers + add gqlgen SetRecoverFunc and schema-walk no-panic test
bead.description:
PROBLEM: 15 root Query resolvers are panic("not implemented") stubs (resolver.go:198-299) while their fields are advertised in schema.graphql and reachable via /graphql + /graphiql; calling them returns an opaque 'internal system error' and spams stderr stack traces. SD-022/FEAT-002 expect every advertised schema operation to work. No SetRecoverFunc exists. Zero test coverage (verified). The web UI consumes NONE of these 15 fields (opus frontend grep) — so most should be TRIMMED, not implemented.
ROOT CAUSE: resolver.go:198-299; no gqlgen recover configured. health/ready/metrics have REST twins (server.go:1252-1285, 3067-3127) so are pure duplication.
PROPOSED FIX: (a) add a gqlgen SetRecoverFunc returning a clean error (stops opaque-500 + stack spam) — the high-value nugget; (b) for each of the 15 fields decide implement-vs-remove using 'is it used by frontend/src?': UI-unused fields (health, ready, metrics*, beadDepTree, doc*, providers, provider) → REMOVE from schema.graphql; only wire fields the UI needs. NOTE: search is NOT covered here — handleSearch is not a valid full-text twin (see the full-text-search bead). NOTE: project-scoped doc GraphQL is a separate bead.
NON-SCOPE: implementing real full-text search; frontend scoping.
bead.acceptance:
1. graphql server installs a SetRecoverFunc that converts resolver panics into structured GraphQL errors (no HTTP 500, no raw stack to client).
2. TestGraphQLNoPanicOnAnyQueryField issues a minimal selection against every root Query field (introspection-driven) and asserts none returns 'internal system error'.
3. UI-unused panicking fields are removed from schema.graphql OR implemented; no panic("not implemented") remains in resolver.go.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... green
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes
